summary: Lead started M6-SUCCESSION-COMPLETION-001 after M6 map candidate pipeline closure entered origin/main. simulation_engineer writer thread 019f0823-18c3-73d1-9314-09f78d7ab0cc is assigned to complete deterministic Alpha succession resolution within task allowed paths.

tests_run.result: Clean main before branch creation; branch feat/m6-succession-completion created from 81e7984ff9a09b889a7cfcf10ac837f54409b21e.
Fetched/pruned origin; main remained up to date.
M6-SUCCESSION-COMPLETION-001 was the only ready task.
Spawned simulation_engineer writer thread 019f0823-18c3-73d1-9314-09f78d7ab0cc, nickname Engine.

decisions: Start M6-SUCCESSION-COMPLETION-001 as the next taskctl READY M6 task after M6-MAP-CANDIDATE-PIPELINE-001 closure.
Keep succession completion deterministic and data-driven; no Math.random/Date.now for authoritative outcomes.
Do not implement M7/M8 content lock, UI, sim-ai, or formal historical content in this task.
Manual node battle remains deferred; no Human Gate is active.
risks: R4 task crosses sim-core, protocol, save-format, and deterministic runtime boundaries; systems_architect review is required before acceptance.
Succession consequences must connect to diplomacy/legitimacy/offices/vassal obligations without duplicating those systems.
requested_action: Complete deterministic Alpha succession resolution within allowed paths, run required tests, and return a simulation_engineer REVIEW handoff for systems_architect review.

acceptance_criteria: M3 succession state machine is completed for Alpha playthroughs with deterministic candidate generation, eligibility, claim strength, legitimacy effects, and auditable resolution events.

Succession completion handles deaths, abdication-like scenario triggers, disputed succession, regency or temporary stewardship if accepted by the M6 contract, and recovery from malformed inputs without data corruption.
All succession commands, queries, events, and save fields are explicit, versioned, serializable schemas with stable IDs and no object-graph serialization.
Succession consequences connect to diplomacy, legitimacy, offices, vassal obligations, and start-to-victory outcomes without duplicating those systems.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover stable heir ordering, no cyclic title/claim references, disputed succession reason codes, save round-trip, and malformed/future payload rejection.
